evidence: File body contains literal escaped text such as mcp\.json, ov\-mcp\-server, Bearer \<API Key\>, \&\#34;启用\&\#34; in prose and table cells
description: Double-escaped backslashes and numeric HTML entities render as garbled literal text instead of clean prose
suggested_fix: Re-export/re-generate this file without double-escaping backslashes and HTML entities

evidence: File body contains the same double-escaped backslash/HTML-entity pattern as zh/cursor.md throughout prose and both tables
description: Same systematic double-escaping defect as zh/cursor.md, likely from a shared export/translation step
suggested_fix: Fix the shared export/translation step and re-check other zh files for the same pattern

evidence: Directory listing of bot/workspace/skills/skill-creator/ contains only SKILL.md; scripts/init_skill.py, scripts/package_skill.py, references/workflows.md, references/output-patterns.md are all absent
description: Skill references four helper files that do not exist anywhere in its directory
suggested_fix: Add the missing scripts/reference docs, or remove the references and inline the guidance

evidence: Directory listing of bot/workspace/skills/opencode/ shows only list_sessions.py and opencode_utils.py; list_messages_of_session.py is absent
description: Skill documents and invokes a helper script that doesn't exist; its own example command appears to be a copy-paste of the list_sessions.py invocation
suggested_fix: Rename the invocation to the existing script, or add the missing list_messages_of_session.py helper

description: Command has no allowed-tools declaration
suggested_fix: Add an allowed-tools frontmatter field listing the tools ov.md is permitted to call

description: Zero example/output-sample blocks demonstrating the command's usage
suggested_fix: Add at least one example invocation with sample output

description: No output-format section describing what the status display returns
suggested_fix: Add an explicit output-format/template section

description: No sample query, candidate JSON, or situation snippet shown anywhere in the skill
suggested_fix: Add at least two worked examples showing input and expected retrieval output

description: Uses "relevant" x2 and "usually" without measurable criteria
suggested_fix: Replace with concrete selection criteria

description: Uses "as needed" and "sufficient" without measurable criteria
suggested_fix: Replace with concrete thresholds/criteria

description: Uses "correctly", "appropriate", "relevant" without measurable criteria
suggested_fix: Replace with concrete criteria

description: Uses "correctly" and "appropriate" without measurable criteria
suggested_fix: Replace with concrete criteria

evidence: allowed-tools lists Glob and Grep but the body never calls either tool
description: Glob and Grep are declared but never referenced in the skill body
suggested_fix: Remove Glob/Grep from allowed-tools, or use them where file discovery is needed

description: Uses "usually" without measurable criteria
suggested_fix: Replace with a concrete condition

description: Uses "relevant" and "correctly" without measurable criteria
suggested_fix: Replace with concrete criteria

description: Uses "relevant" x4, "usually", "correctly" without measurable criteria
suggested_fix: Replace with concrete criteria throughout

description: Uses "relevant" x3, "effectively", "correctly" without measurable criteria
suggested_fix: Replace with concrete criteria throughout

description: Uses "as needed" x5, "appropriate", "relevant" without measurable criteria
suggested_fix: Replace with concrete criteria throughout

evidence: codex-memory-plugin/hooks/hooks.json uses "matcher": "*" at lines 17, 29, 41 while the sibling claude-code-memory-plugin/hooks/hooks.json uses "" for the same match-all intent
description: Two parallel memory plugins use different matcher idioms for the same match-all behavior
suggested_fix: Standardize on the empty-string "" match-all idiom across both plugins

description: zh version's embedded prompt is less explicit than en's about substituting the placeholder with a real API key value
suggested_fix: Align zh phrasing with en's explicit substitution instruction

description: zh says "copy the following API Key" with no key value shown, while en and both languages of hermes.md/openclaw.md say "shown on the page"
suggested_fix: Align zh/cli.md phrasing with the "shown on the page" pattern used elsewhere

evidence: subprocess.run(["bash", "-c", "curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh"], check=False) inside install_ollama(), macOS branch
description: Runtime execution of an unpinned remote install script with no checksum verification, triggered during normal CLI operation
suggested_fix: Pin/verify a checksum for install.sh, or require the user to run the official installer manually instead of auto-invoking it

evidence: subprocess.run(["bash", "-c", "curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh"], check=False) inside install_ollama(), Linux branch
description: Same unpinned remote-script execution pattern on the Linux code path
suggested_fix: Same as the macOS branch: pin/verify a checksum or require manual install

evidence: curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_20.x | bash - inside a RUN instruction
description: Build-time execution of NodeSource's remote setup script via shell pipe
suggested_fix: Vendor a pinned/checksummed NodeSource setup script, or use the distro package manager where possible

evidence: curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh inside the same RUN instruction
description: Build-time execution of astral.sh's uv installer via shell pipe
suggested_fix: Pin uv installation to a specific release artifact with checksum verification

evidence: curl -fsSL https://deb.nodesource.com/setup_20.x | bash - inside a RUN instruction, duplicate of bot/deploy/Dockerfile
description: Same NodeSource setup pattern duplicated in the second deployment Dockerfile
suggested_fix: Same as bot/deploy/Dockerfile:7; consider consolidating the two Dockerfiles

evidence: curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh inside the same RUN instruction, duplicate of bot/deploy/Dockerfile
description: Same uv-installer pattern duplicated in the second deployment Dockerfile
suggested_fix: Same as bot/deploy/Dockerfile:10

evidence: curl -fsSL http://openviking.tos-cn-beijing.volces.com/cli/install.sh | bash in a CI workflow step
description: CI installs the project's own CLI via curl-pipe-bash over plain HTTP with no signature check, from a first-party domain
suggested_fix: Serve install.sh over HTTPS and verify a checksum/signature before piping to bash

evidence: subprocess.run(cmd, shell=True, text=True, ...) inside execute_cmd(), a generic command-string executor
description: Unsandboxed shell=True executor backing an LLM-driven bot skill; the command string can originate from model output
suggested_fix: Switch to argument-list subprocess calls without shell=True, or run through the project's existing sandbox backend

evidence: subprocess call with shell=True in an e2e test file
description: shell=True call site in test-only code, not part of the production execution path
suggested_fix: Use argument-list form even in test code to avoid setting a shell=True precedent

description: Captured conversation turns (possibly containing user-pasted secrets) are POSTed to cfg.baseUrl, default localhost but overridable via OPENVIKING_URL
suggested_fix: Warn or refuse at startup if OPENVIKING_URL is set to a non-localhost http:// origin

description: Same egress pattern; request also attaches Authorization/X-API-Key headers built from cfg.apiKey
suggested_fix: Same fix, plus redact/omit X-API-Key when baseUrl isn't localhost

description: exec(sandboxedCommand) after SandboxManager.wrapWithSandbox() — intended purpose of this sandbox backend
suggested_fix: No action needed if SandboxManager's wrapping is verified to prevent escape; otherwise audit the sandbox boundary

description: Second exec(sandboxedCommand) call site in the same backend (executeCommandInternal)
suggested_fix: Same as line 202

evidence: Read bin/postinstall.mjs in full: it only calls execFileSync(cmd, [...args, "--version"]) for diagnostics and process.stderr.write for a usage banner; no network calls, no download-and-execute
description: postinstall script present but reviewed as benign version/PATH diagnostics only
fp_reason: Content verified line-by-line; no download-execute, no network call, only local --version checks and a printed usage banner
rule_gap: Postinstall-presence pattern matching should distinguish diagnostic-only scripts (execFileSync with fixed args, no network) from actual install/fetch logic
suggested_fix: Document in README/SECURITY.md that postinstall only prints diagnostics, to pre-empt scanner false positives

evidence: "matcher": "*" at lines 17, 29, 41 for UserPromptSubmit/Stop/PreCompact
description: Broad wildcard matcher fires on every event for three hook types, vs. sibling plugin's scoped empty-string idiom
suggested_fix: Align on the "" match-all idiom used by claude-code-memory-plugin/hooks/hooks.json

evidence: "@tanstack/react-devtools": "latest" (also lines 47, 48, 81, 82, 83)
description: 6 @tanstack/* dependencies pinned to "latest" instead of a resolvable version
suggested_fix: Pin to explicit resolved versions

evidence: "@base-ui/react": "^1.3.0" and similar caret ranges throughout the dependencies block
description: Majority of dependencies use caret ranges, permitting automatic minor/patch drift
suggested_fix: Adopt a lockfile-enforced CI check if reproducibility is a concern; caret ranges alone are standard practice

evidence: requests>=2.28.0
description: Unpinned lower-bound-only version range with no upper bound
suggested_fix: Add an upper bound or switch to a lockfile

evidence: All 9 listed dependencies use >= with no upper bound
description: All dependencies in this requirements file are unbounded
suggested_fix: Add upper bounds or switch to a lockfile
